title: Concorda dependency-graph reverse index
description: Materialized inverse of every node's depends_on across the corpus. One file per regen, owned by reconcile.py. Per-node JSON files do NOT carry dependents; this file is the single source of truth for reverse lookups, so a regen that changes only edges does not touch any node file. Written atomically (tmp + rename).

schema_version.description: Bump when the index format changes incompatibly. Hooks refuse to read mismatched versions.

generated_at.description: When this index was last rebuilt. The hook can use this to detect stale indexes vs current node state, but the index is the cache, not the truth — regen rebuilds it deterministically from depends_on.

git_commit.description: HEAD commit at index generation time. Read by drift checks; not load-bearing for correctness.

node_count.description: Total nodes considered when building this index. A drop here vs the prior generation is a signal that an extractor failed silently.

edge_count.description: Total reverse edges in the index.

by_target.description: Map of target node id → list of incoming edges. The list is sorted deterministically (by source id, then via, then where) so this file is bit-stable across regens that produce the same edge set.

source.description: id of the node that depends on the target
